destinations.evidence: Delivery result submission accepts only AwaitingDelivery and rejects Fulfilled before any delivery result is stored.
The separate canonical-settlement path transitions Fulfilled to AwaitingDelivery and releases the sender lock.
The pool invokes mark_awaiting_delivery only while processing canonical Phase 1 settlement transactions.

equivalence_argument: Delivery submission is gated on canonical AwaitingDelivery, while the canonical Phase 1 settlement processor performs the only Fulfilled-to-AwaitingDelivery transition.
rationale: The diff replaces a recovery auto-transition from Fulfilled to AwaitingDelivery with warnings and rejection, explaining that Phase 1 settlement must be canonicalized before delivery submission to avoid consensus divergence. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Async delivery results are accepted only for transactions in AwaitingDelivery state; submitting delivery while the transaction is still Fulfilled is rejected rather than auto-transitioned, and AwaitingDelivery is set only after Phase 1 settlement is canonicalized.
suggested_falsification_tests: Insert a long-running async transaction in Fulfilled state and call submit_delivery_result, asserting it is rejected; then simulate canonical finalization that marks the entry AwaitingDelivery and assert the same delivery path becomes eligible.
summary: Delivery waits for canonical Phase 1 settlement before accepting Phase 2 results.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Insert a Fulfilled two-phase entry, assert delivery submission rejects it, then process a canonical Phase 1 settlement and assert the entry becomes AwaitingDelivery.
title: Delivery waits for finalized Phase 1 settlement
